tavily.raw: {"query":"Berkshire Hathaway 2026 Alphabet private placement $10 billion AI infrastructure investment","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/06/01/berkshire-hathaway-alphabet-investment.html","title":"Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alphabet, deepening bet on AI","content":"# Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alphabet, deepening bet on AI. Berkshire Hathaway is deepening its bet on artificial intelligence and one of the industry's dominant players, investing an additional $10 billion in Alphabet through a private stock purchase. Alphabet said Monday it reached an agreement to sell $5 billion of its Class A shares to Berkshire at $351.81 apiece and another $5 billion of Class C stock at $348.20 per share. The latest purchase signals Berkshire's growing conviction in Alphabet's position at the center of the AI boom, spanning search, cloud computing and digital infrastructure. It also offers an early glimpse into CEO Greg Abel's capital allocation approach, suggesting Warren Buffett's successor is willing to commit significant sums to tech companies as Berkshire seeks new avenues for deploying its nearly $400 billion cash pile at the end of March. The company said it plans to use the proceeds for general corporate purposes, including capital expenditures to expand its AI infrastructure and global computing capacity.","score":0.94933707,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/alphabet-announces-80b-fund-raise-221301965.html","title":"Alphabet Announces $80B Fund Raise, Berkshire ...","content":"# Alphabet Announces $80B Fund Raise, Berkshire Hathaway To Invest $10B In Private Placement. Alphabet Announces $80B Fund Raise, Berkshire Hathaway To Invest $10B In Private Placement · Stocktwits. * The plan includes $30B in underwritten offerings and a $40B at-the-market program to raise capital over time. * Berkshire Hathaway will invest $10B in a private placement split between Class A and Class C shares. (GOOGL) announced a plan to raise $80 billion in equity capital, a massive move aimed at bankrolling the skyrocketing costs of its artificial intelligence infrastructure. It also includes a $40 billion at-the-market (ATM) program for Class A and Class C shares, expected to begin in Q3 2026. Separately, Alphabet has agreed to sell $10 billion in stock to Berkshire Hathaway through a private placement. Alphabet said proceeds from the offerings and private placement will be used for general corporate purposes, including capital spending to expand AI infrastructure and global compute capacity.","score":0.92588276,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/05/berkshire-hathaway-just-put-10-billion-into-alphab","title":"Berkshire Hathaway Just Agreed to Put $10 Billion Into Alphabet's AI Build-Out. Should Investors Follow? | The Motley Fool","content":"It isn't every day that one of the most profitable companies on the planet asks investors for cash. But that is effectively what **Alphabet** (GOOG 2.17%)(GOOGL 2.05%) did earlier this week, announcing a $84.75 billion equity capital raise -- the largest equity raise in U.S. corporate history -- to help fund its enormous artificial intelligence (AI) build-out. In a notable show of support, Warren Buffett's **Berkshire Hathaway** (BRKA 0.34%)(BRKB 0.42%) agreed to buy $10 billion of that stock through a private placement. And Berkshire's commitment is one of the bigger bets new CEO Greg Abel has made since taking the reins from Buffett at the start of the year. On its first-quarter earnings call in late April, Alphabet lifted its 2026 capital spending guidance to a range of $180 billion to $190 billion, up from a prior range of $175 billion to $185 billion -- and said 2027 spending should increase significantly from there.","score":0.9220975,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261941047-alphabet-goog-berkshire-stake-greg-abel-ai-capex-dilution-risk-cloud-revenue-tradingkey","title":"Berkshire Bets Another $10 Billion on AI, Why Are Investors Voting With Their Feet?","content":"Alphabet plans an $80 billion equity financing for AI infrastructure, with Berkshire Hathaway subscribing to $10 billion. This marks Berkshire's largest tech investment under CEO Greg Abel's more aggressive capital allocation. Alphabet's shares declined post-announcement, with investors concerned about short-term dilution from the financing, particularly the $40 billion At-The-Market offering. The shift from operating cash flow to equity offerings for capital expenditures suggests a longer wait for AI profitability. Following Alphabet's announcement of the massive $80 billion financing, its shares fell more than 2% in after-hours trading to approximately $363, before narrowing the losses slightly.
